halmos.tool: Halmos (a16z) — symbolic execution of the REAL contract, no model in between

checks.name: check_I2_depositPreservesBook
checks.claim: A deposit mints the SHFI share one-to-one with the capital contributed — for ANY amount.

checks.name: check_I2_exitPreservesBook
checks.claim: An exit — after the due notice the clause requires — burns exactly what it pays out and leaves every other partner's stake untouched, for ANY amount.

checks.name: check_I2_transferPreservesBook
checks.claim: A share transfer moves stake between partners and creates none — supply and books stay equal for ANY amount (transfers open only after commencement of activity).

checks.name: check_I3_accrualMonotone
checks.claim: The profit accumulator only ever grows: distributed profit is never clawed back, for ANY revenue.

checks.name: check_I6_writeOffProRata
checks.claim: A write-off cuts every partner's stake EXACTLY in proportion to capital — stated as an equality: a 1-wei drift in the pool's favour would be a counterexample. Proved for every repayment the contract accepts.

checks.name: check_slashRestoresProRata
checks.claim: A post-write-off board verdict restores every partner's stake EXACTLY pro-rata — for ANY verdict amount, after the execute-time caps; the collateral freeze is fully released.

checks.name: check_writeOffNetsRevenue
checks.claim: A write-off first nets undistributed revenue into the shortfall as capital recovery: no manager fee, no owner cut, loss shrinks by exactly the netted amount — for ANY revenue.

bounds: Proofs hold for all inputs AT THIS POOL SHAPE: 3 investors, write-off loop unrolled to 4 — not for pools of every size.
repaid/revenue are uint64 — no generality lost: the contract rejects anything above the 10-ether project, and 64-bit division is what keeps the solver from timing out.
I1 (solvency), I4 (profit only with capital intact) and I5 (frozen vote weights) stay with the fuzzer — they depend on contract balance and voting history, not one operation's arithmetic.

mutationsCaught.mutation: write-off splits the loss equally instead of pro-rata
transfer credits the recipient without debiting the sender

mutationsCaught.counterexample: concrete revenue value; the other five checks stay green

mutationsCaught.mutation: verdict restoration splits equally instead of pro-rata
mutationsCaught.counterexample: concrete verdict amount, 20 paths; the other six checks stay green
